Busselton · WA South West

A bakery that comes to you.

Pastries made and baked fresh every morning, then loaded into the van and brought to the markets and holiday parks of the South West. Listen out for the bell.

Where to find us

The week's rounds

We're a mobile bakery — no fixed shopfront just yet. Here's where the van and the market stalls turn up. Times can shift, so check our socials before you set out.

🔔
This week School-holiday rounds are on — we're visiting the holiday parks every morning except Sunday. Catch us at the Busselton Foreshore Market this Sunday.

Out and about at festivals too — find us at the Telling Tales Festival and the Medieval Festival in Balingup. Want us at your event? Get in touch.

From the oven

What Sam bakes

Hand-crafted each morning before sale — buttery croissants, delicate danish, savoury pies and quiche, and a rotating selection of naturally leavened sourdough.

Croissants

Plain, chocolate, almond, ham & cheese, cinnamon — laminated and baked golden each morning.

Danish & pastries

Pain au raisin, snails and seasonal pastries with that glossy, flaky finish.

Sweet treats

Fruit danish, brownies and friands — including gluten-free options.

Our story

Stoke-on-Trent to the South West

Stoke Bakery began with a simple idea: bake good bread and pastry, and take it straight to the people who'll eat it.

Head baker and owner Sam Lennon was born in Stoke-on-Trent and lived between Dunedin, Perth and Sydney before settling his passion for baking in Busselton. He trained in patisserie and bread at La Patisserie in South Perth, then sharpened his craft at AP Bakery in Sydney — and now runs a bakery of his own.

The commercial bakery in Busselton is fully solar-powered, and we use local ingredients wherever we can. Good baking you can feel good about.

— Sam Lennon, Head Baker
